normalized_title: Position Statement: Housing on Lots Less than 100m2
version_date: 8 March 2024 (published 8 March 2024; supersedes first version of June 2019)
scope_summary: WAPC position statement providing interim locational guidance, in conjunction with R-Codes Volume 1, for subdivision and house design on residential lots less than 100m2 across Western Australia. It applies to standard structure plans, precinct structure plans and scheme amendments that include such lots, requiring the R100-SL density code, locational criteria (proximity to centres and public open space, transition between density bands) and siting/configuration criteria (street frontage, corner sites, building height). Housing on lots under 100m2 must not be the predominant housing type in a street and must be delivered through comprehensive planning.

key_numeric_standards.topic: lot size threshold for policy application
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: single house and grouped dwelling lots covered by this position statement
subdivision in areas coded R100-SL (no average lot size requirement)
areas identified for housing on lots less than 100m2 in structure plans / scheme amendments

key_numeric_standards.topic: distance to secondary or district centre
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: lots less than 100m2 relative to the core/largest commercial landholding of an existing or proposed secondary or district centre

key_numeric_standards.topic: distance to local or neighbourhood centre
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: lots less than 100m2 relative to the core/largest commercial landholding of any existing or proposed local or neighbourhood centre
lots less than 100m2 relative to local or neighbourhood public open space, preferably fronting or overlooking it
lots less than 100m2 relative to the core/largest commercial landholding of any strategic metropolitan centre
pedestrian footpath on the street or public right-of-way fronted by lots less than 100m2
dwellings on lots less than 100m2 (refer R-Codes Volume 1 Part C, C2.10)
structure plans approved under the June 2019 first version before the R100-SL code existed; conforming lots <100m2 may continue to be created

cross_references.cited: future Neighbourhood Design State Planning Policy

page numbering artifact: page 1 header extracted as '11'
